findings.citation: process: no telemetry event for 664aaf60 in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/.runtime/refinery-telemetry/refinery-gate-20260624.jsonl; no gate log at /home/ubuntu/gt-town/.runtime/refinery-gate-logs/ in 19:10Z-19:59Z window
findings.explanation: Bypass-merge: commit 664aaf60 reached origin/main with zero gate telemetry. The committer date is 2026-06-24T19:39:50Z; the surrounding gate logs are at 19:10:04Z and 19:59:12Z, with no entry between them. There is no event for 664aaf60 in refinery-gate-20260624.jsonl (295 lines, grep returns no matches). Per the strict four-model rule (m3 / codex / umans-kimi / umans-glm) established in gastown-cet.12.4, every merge to main must have a recorded gate panel. This is strictly worse than the gastown-cet.12.4 degraded-quorum case. Follow-up: file bead to require a CI failure when a commit reaches main without a gate record, and to investigate the refinery pipeline for the 2026-06-24T19:10Z-19:59Z gap.

findings.citation: commit subject 'for new gtviz rig'; diff in /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gastown/.beads/config.yaml
findings.explanation: Misleading subject: the commit says 'add explicit shared Dolt config keys for new gtviz rig' but the change is in the gastown root .beads/config.yaml. The gtviz rig was bootstrapped six days later (gtviz .beads/config.yaml was created 2026-06-30T19:58:43-04:00 per stat(1), six days after this commit landed at 2026-06-24T19:39:50Z). The gtviz bootstrap independently wrote the same three keys (storage.backend: dolt, dolt.server: 127.0.0.1, dolt.port: 3307), so the change in 664aaf60 was effectively redundant for the gtviz rig and only meaningful for the gastown root's doctor-check. The commit message does not acknowledge this. Follow-up: file bead to require commit-message scope review.

No tests added. The change is to a key set the doctor check (internal/doctor/dolt_config_check.go) requires; no test in internal/doctor/dolt_config_check_test.go was updated to assert the gastown-root key set is recognized. Follow-up: file bead to add a test.

findings.citation: gastown-cet.12.4 strict four-model rule not applied at landing time
findings.explanation: Process violation: the gastown-cet.12.4 audit established a strict four-model rule (m3 / codex / umans-kimi / umans-glm) for re-reviews of merged commits. Commit 664aaf60 (landing 2026-06-24T19:39:50Z) was merged without any record of any reviewer being invoked — strictly worse than the gastown-cet.12.4 degraded-quorum case. Follow-up: file bead to enforce the four-model rule at merge time and reject commits that lack a recorded gate panel.

Quote-style inconsistency: dolt.server: "127.0.0.1" is quoted but dolt.port: 3307 is bare. The doctor check parser (configYAMLValues at internal/doctor/dolt_config_check.go:204) trims quotes, so both work, but the asymmetry is jarring. Pre-existing config style also mixes; this is a low-priority cleanup. Follow-up: include in any rework commit.

summary: FAIL — On the corrected facts (v1 had pointed to a non-existent /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gtviz/.repo.git/.beads/config.yaml and claimed gtviz uses a different port; v2 verifies F1 the active gtviz beads dir is /home/ubuntu/gt-town/gtviz/.beads/ and F2 gtviz uses port 3307), the change in 664aaf60 is still a strict bypass-merge. The single BLOCKING is zero gate telemetry. The MAJOR findings are: misleading commit message (subject says gtviz, diff is in gastown root), no tests added, and the gastown-cet.12.4 four-model rule was not applied at landing time. The MINOR is the quote-style inconsistency. Required: investigate the refinery pipeline for the 2026-06-24T19:10Z-19:59Z gap, enforce the four-model rule at merge time, and require a gate record for any future merge to main.
